name: The Hole - PC Quarters
summary: A private room in the basement of The Hole Setite temple/nightclub in Mesa, connected via utility tunnel to a nearby apartment complex, providing discrete access and escape routes for a Setite PC.
description:
Your room is in the deepest level of The Hole, carved from what was once the building's utility infrastructure before the Setites claimed it. The space is perhaps ten by twelve feet, its walls painted a deep burgundy that seems to absorb light, making the room feel smaller and more intimate than it actually is. Gold serpentine patterns are stenciled along the baseboards and ceiling edges, their sinuous curves suggesting movement even when the light is still. A single lamp with a red silk shade casts warm, amber light that makes shadows dance along the walls. The floor is polished concrete, cool beneath your feet, and the ceiling is low—maybe eight feet—giving the space a cocoon-like quality. A simple bed occupies one corner, its frame dark wood carved with subtle Egyptian motifs, its sheets black silk that feels cool against your skin. A small altar sits against the opposite wall, its surface of black basalt holding a few ritual implements: a bronze censer for incense, a small chalice, and a carving of a serpent coiled around an ankh. The air carries the scent of sandalwood and myrrh, mixed with something else—something metallic and coppery that lingers from recent rituals performed elsewhere in the temple.

Two doors lead out of your room, both appearing to be simple utility access points but actually serving as escape routes. The first opens into a narrow utility tunnel that runs beneath the street, connecting The Hole's basement to the utility basement of a three-story apartment complex one block away. The tunnel is perhaps four feet wide and six feet tall, its walls concrete, its floor damp with condensation. Exposed pipes run along the ceiling, dripping occasionally, and the air is thick with the smell of old water and something else—something that might be mold or might be something older, something that remembers when this land was desert before the city was built. The tunnel is monitored by electronic sensors—motion detectors, pressure plates, cameras hidden in the shadows—all feeding back to The Hole's security systems. The second door leads to the same tunnel from a different angle, providing redundancy in case one route is compromised.

The psychological weight of living here isn't in the room itself—it's in what surrounds it and what it connects to. The Hole operates on multiple levels: the public nightclub above, where mortals come to lose themselves in music and drink and the promise of something more; the private levels below, where Setites conduct their business and their worship; and the deepest basements, where the true work happens—the corruption, the rituals, the slow turning of souls toward Set's purpose. You're part of that machine, a small cog in a larger mechanism of seduction and decay. The apartment complex one block away provides cover, a place to blend in, to appear normal. But the tunnel that connects you to it is more than just an escape route—it's a reminder that you're always connected, always part of something larger, always serving a purpose that extends far beyond these walls.

But the most unsettling thing about this haven isn't the security or the connections or even the proximity to the temple's darker functions. It's the knowledge that you're sleeping in a space that has been consecrated, however subtly, to Set. The gold serpentine patterns aren't just decoration—they're symbols, and symbols have power. The incense that drifts through the air from other parts of the temple carries with it the weight of rituals performed, of prayers offered, of corruption invited. And sometimes, in the deepest hours of the night, when The Hole is quiet except for the distant thump of bass from the club above and the whisper of air through the ventilation system, you can feel it—the sense that something is watching, that the darkness itself is aware, that every choice you make, every action you take, is being weighed and measured against Set's ancient purpose. The tunnel to the apartment complex might offer escape from physical danger, but there's no escaping what you are, what you serve, or what the temple has made of you. The corruption isn't just around you—it's in the very walls, in the air you breathe, in the symbols that surround you. And in the end, the only real question is whether you're using the corruption, or whether it's using you.
notes: Potential haven if PC is of that Clan. Located in the basement of The Hole Setite temple/nightclub in Mesa. Connected via utility tunnel to a three-story apartment complex one block away. PC has two exits from the room into the tunnel, then exits through the apartment complex utility room, through the laundry room, and out the front door. Electronic security monitors the tunnel and laundry room. The Hole itself has heavy security. This is a sub-location of The Hole (Setite Temple/Nightclub), providing discrete access and escape routes while maintaining connection to the temple. The apartment complex serves as cover and provides a way to blend in with mortal society.
status_notes: Operational as part of The Hole complex. Room maintained according to temple standards. Access privileges subject to Setite hierarchy and good standing within the clan.
address: Basement level, The Hole (Setite Temple/Nightclub) - exact address classified
owner_notes: Part of The Hole complex owned and controlled by Roxanne Murphy (Setite temple-mistress). PC has residence rights as a clan member but does not own the room or facility. Access and privileges are granted based on clan hierarchy and standing within the Followers of Set.
access_notes: Access controlled by Roxanne Murphy and temple security protocols. PC has access to: personal room (basement level), utility tunnel (two exits from room), apartment complex utility room, apartment complex laundry room, and apartment complex front door. Access to other areas of The Hole subject to temple hierarchy and security clearances. Electronic security monitors tunnel and laundry room. Heavy security throughout The Hole temple/nightclub. The apartment complex provides discrete access and escape routes.
security_notes: Heavy security in The Hole temple/nightclub. PC room is in basement level with two exits into utility tunnel. Electronic security monitors tunnel (motion detectors, pressure plates, hidden cameras). Electronic security on apartment complex laundry room. The Hole itself has extensive security including guards, cameras, alarms, and ritual warding. Room is completely sealed against sunlight. Utility tunnel provides discrete access and escape route. Apartment complex serves as cover and provides additional security through anonymity. All security features of The Hole apply to this sub-location.
utility_notes: Blood storage: Small refrigeration unit in room stores emergency blood supplies. Hidden caches: Supplies and emergency gear stored in room and throughout tunnel access points. Communications: Phone line access, connection to The Hole's communication networks. No computers, library, medical facilities, or specialized equipment in room itself—these are available elsewhere in The Hole complex. The utility tunnel and apartment complex provide additional storage and access options.
social_features: PC room is part of larger Hole complex that serves as social hub for Phoenix Setites. The nightclub above provides public social space. Private levels below serve for Setite gatherings and rituals. PC can participate in temple social activities but room itself is private space. Capacity for social functions is extensive (full temple/nightclub), but PC's personal space is limited to small room. Social standing within clan determines access to various temple areas and information.
ritual_space: Small altar in room for personal rituals. Access to larger ritual spaces in The Hole subject to temple hierarchy. The room itself is subtly consecrated to Set through symbols and ritual implements. The temple's deeper levels contain extensive ritual spaces for Akhu rites and Setite worship.
magical_protection: Inherits from The Hole overall magical protection. Ritual warding protects the temple against supernatural intrusion. The room's consecration to Set provides subtle protective effects. The symbols and ritual implements create layers of magical protection. The utility tunnel may have additional protective measures known only to temple hierarchy.
cursed_blessed: Living in The Hole is both blessing and burden. Access to resources, protection, and connection to Setite power, but also constant awareness of corruption, temple hierarchy, and the weight of serving Set's purpose. The room's consecration provides protection but also binds you to the temple's purpose. The corruption isn't just around you—it's in the very walls, in the air you breathe, in the symbols that surround you. The only real question is whether you're using the corruption, or whether it's using you.
relationship_notes: PC quarters are a private room in the basement of The Hole Setite temple/nightclub. Connected via utility tunnel to a three-story apartment complex one block away. Two exits from room into tunnel, then through apartment utility room, laundry room, and out front door. Electronic security on tunnel and laundry room. Heavy security in The Hole. The apartment complex provides discrete access and escape routes. This is a sub-location of The Hole, though The Hole itself may not yet be fully documented as a location entry.
